titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 586

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=838 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 15:48:09.487676+00 | translation moa=839 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 15:48:09.499086+00

p.cl:
. W . dei gratia Rex Scott’. Episcopis . Abbatibus . Comitibus . Baronibus . Iusticiis. vicecomitibus . Prepositis . Ministris . et Omnibus probis Hominibus Totius terre sue
Clericis et Laicis Salutem .

me concessisse et Hac Carta mea confirmasse Amicabilem pacem et compositionem factam
inter . Willelmum . Episcopum Sancti Andree . et Priorem et Conuentum Dunelmensem . et Ecclesiam de Coldingham . Super Cano et Cuneueto . et
procurationibus et Hospitiis et Custodiis . et institutionibus Ecclesiarum ad Predictos Priorem et Conuentum Dunelmensem . in Episcopatu Sancti Andree
pertinentium . et processionem et uisitationem Earundem Ecclesiarum . Quare uolo et firmiter precipio ut prefata pax et Compositio inter eos facta
super predictis  futuris perpetuo temporibus firmiter et inuiolabiliter obseruetur sicut Scriptum inter eos inde factum testatur .

Testibus . Willelmo . abbate
de Melros . Comite . Patricio . Iusticio . Alano filio . Rollandi . Constabulario . Philipo de Valoniiis . Camerario . meo . Roberto . de Londoniis . filio meo . Willelmo .
Cumin . Willelmo . Capellano meo . Willelmo . de Bosco . Alexandro . vicecomite . de Striuelin . Magistro Nicholao . Magistro Martino . Medicis . Gilberto . de
Striuelin . DauiD . Marescallo . Walter Murdac . Willelmo . Olifard .
William, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to bishops, abbots, earls, barons, justices, sheriffs, provosts, officials, and all worthy men of his entire land, clerk and lay, greeting.

me to have approved, and by this my charter to have made firm, the friendly peace and agreement made between William the bishop of St Andrews, and the prior and monastic community of Durham and the church of Coldingham, over the cain, conveth, procurations, hospitality, and the care of and appointments to churches belonging to the aforesaid prior and monastic community of Durham in the bishopric of St Andrews, and the episcopal progression and visitation of the same churches. Wherefore I will and resolutely instruct that the aforementioned peace and agreement made between them over the aforesaid should in future times be perpetually observed, strictly and inviolably, just as the document produced thereupon between them bears witness.
At Wedale on the 16th day of May.
